methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rainmaker is a Series A stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector founded by Augustus Doricko. The company has raised $25M to date. The broader sector includes 8 private comparable companies, among them Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Dronamics. No public peers with sufficient comparability exist in this space.

Given the early-stage and heterogeneous nature of this sector, only one valuation method yielded reliable output. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement, applied to the $25M raised as a baseline proxy for pre-money valuation. This method produces an implied valuation of $50M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable; while 4 same-sector companies exist, filtering by stage, adjacent-stage timing, and valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making a sector-median approach unreliably narrow and potentially misleading.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, with a median point of $50M. This range reflects ±15% around the stage-progression midpoint to account for company-specific execution risk and market conditions. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrowly comparable Series A-stage drones companies with disclosed valuations and the wide dispersion in business models and commercialization timelines across the sector. Any refinement would require either additional private market data on same-stage peers or explicit founder guidance on investor return expectations.
